lead:
**Libre Cloud** is a zero-trust self-hosted infrastructure project running on Oracle Cloud Always Free tier. The design routes all external traffic through Cloudflare for DDoS protection and IP masking, then into an Oracle Cloud VM running a Caddy reverse proxy that serves public and WireGuard-private services.

The project name reflects the philosophy behind it: all services are free and open source, the platform is free-tier cloud, and the domain name carries the FOSS identity. It connects directly to the [[privacy-stack|Privacy Stack]] — services like Vaultwarden (passwords), Joplin Server (notes), and SearXNG (private search) are all designed to run in this infrastructure.

content.title: Status: Blocked / Severely Constrained
content.content: The target VM was VM.Standard.A1.Flex (4 OCPU, 24 GB RAM — ARM Ampere, free forever on OCI). Persistent 'out of capacity' errors in the Hyderabad region made this unachievable after repeated attempts. The plan to get that VM is abandoned for now. What actually exists: 1x VM.Standard.E2.1.Micro — 1 OCPU, 1 GB RAM.

The zero-trust architecture is designed so that no service is directly exposed to the internet without passing through Cloudflare and the reverse proxy layer. Services are split into three visibility tiers: public (accessible to anyone via Cloudflare), private (accessible only through a WireGuard VPN tunnel), and ARM-only (requiring the unprovisioned A1.Flex VM).

content.title: Architecture flow
content.content: Internet → Cloudflare (DNS + DDoS + IP masking) → Oracle Cloud VM (1 GB Micro) → Reverse proxy (Caddy/nginx) → [Public: Portfolio, Pi-hole DoH] / [Private (WireGuard-gated): Vaultwarden, Joplin Server, Gitea]

rows.Purpose: Requires 24 GB RAM — indefinitely on hold

content.content:
Oracle Cloud's Always Free tier is uniquely generous compared to AWS, GCP, and Azure free tiers. The headline offering is the **ARM Ampere A1.Flex** instance: 4 OCPU and 24 GB RAM — free forever, no expiry.

For context, AWS Free Tier gives a t2.micro (1 vCPU, 1 GB) for 12 months only. Oracle's AMD Micro is a comparable shape but is always-free in perpetuity, and the ARM Ampere would be dramatically more capable.

The catch is capacity availability: the Hyderabad region ARM Ampere has been perennially 'out of capacity'. Other regions would require a separate account setup. This is a known, widely-reported issue in the Oracle Cloud community — not unique to this project.
The plan is sound. The hardware just never materialised.
content.author: Project notes, April 2026
